name: preconsistency vector
statement_text:
A general linear method (A, U, B, V ) is ‘preconsistent’ if
there exists a vector u such that
                                       V u = u,                              (510a)
                                       U u = 1.                              (510b)
The vector u is the ‘preconsistency vector’.
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
A general linear method is defined by four matrices $A$, $U$, $B$, $V$ and operates on a vector of approximations from the previous step. The method's behavior is characterized by vectors $u$ and $v$, where $u$ relates to the representation of the solution and its derivative in the input approximations. Here $h$ is the step size and $y^{[n-1]}$ is the vector of approximations from the preceding step. The $i$th subvector in $y^{[n-1]}$ represents $u_i y(x_{n-1}) + v_i h y'(x_{n-1}) + O(h^2)$.

variables.name: A
variables.definition: One of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: U
variables.definition: One of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: B
variables.definition: One of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: V
variables.definition: One of the four defining matrices of a general linear method

variables.name: u
variables.definition: A vector characteristic of the method, such that the ith subvector in y^{[n-1]} represents u_i y(x_{n-1}) + v_i h y'(x_{n-1}) + O(h^2)

variables.name: v
variables.definition: A vector characteristic of the method, related to u and the method matrices

variables.name: y^{[n-1]}
variables.definition: Vector of approximations from the preceding step

preamble: A general linear method is defined by four matrices (A, U, B, V) and operates on a vector of approximations from the previous step. The method's behavior is characterized by vectors u and v, where u relates to the representation of the solution and its derivative in the input approximations.
